retrieval_text: Burdock is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 200 works and 433 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Burdock. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Seed, Bark, Leaf, Flower, Berry. Common preparation co-mentions include Tea, Decoction, Syrup, Powder, Oil, Tincture. Common condition co-mentions include Wounds or ulcers, Cold or catarrh, Rheumatism, Fever, Epilepsy or convulsions, Inflammation. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Bleeding caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Fatal-risk warning. Representative source works include Dr. Mansperger's book of hygiene : containing instructions for the cure of diseases, together with many valuable and useful prescriptions : added to which is an accurate description of the properties and application of the curative powers of roots and herbs, with instructions for a diagnosis of diseases from the urine and pulse; The American family physician, or, Domestic guide to health : prepared expressly for the use of families, in language adapted to the understanding of the people : arranged in two divisions; The magnetic and botanic family physician, and domestic practice of natural medicine : with illustrations showing various phases of mesmeric treatment, including full and concise instruction in mesmerism, curative magnetism, massage, and medical botany / by D. Younger.; The occult family physician and botanic guide to health : comprising a description of many American and foreign plants, and their medical virtues, with the cause, cure, and prevention of disease : to which is added, an explanation of the hidden forces in nature, with a large number of valuable receipts, the experience of twenty years' practice. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
